journal_entry.description: Refinance bank loan — old note replaced by new note
journal_entry.rationale: This is a straightforward debt refinancing where the old $100 loan is extinguished and simultaneously replaced by a new $100 loan. The debit removes the old liability (liability decrease), and the credit establishes the new liability (liability increase). Since both notes are stated at $100, there is no gain or loss on extinguishment. The transaction text indicates a clean replacement with no fees or additional costs mentioned. This is a balance sheet transaction with no income statement impact.

disambiguator_responses.aspect: interest rate or terms of new loan
disambiguator_responses.reason: Interest rate and terms affect future accruals, not the refinancing entry itself. Both notes are stated at $100, so the entry simply extinguishes one liability and records another at the same amount.

disambiguator_responses.aspect: whether any fees or costs were incurred
disambiguator_responses.reason: Transaction explicitly states 'old $100 note replaced by new $100 note' with no mention of fees. If fees existed, they would be reflected in the stated amounts or mentioned separately.

disambiguator_responses.aspect: whether there was any gain or loss on extinguishment
disambiguator_responses.reason: Both notes are stated at $100, meaning carrying amount equals settlement amount. No gain or loss arises from this refinancing.

ambiguities.aspect: interest rate or terms of new loan
ambiguities.resolution: Interest rate and terms don't affect the refinancing entry itself — old liability is extinguished at $100, new liability is recorded at $100. Terms affect future interest accruals, not this transaction.

ambiguities.aspect: whether any fees or costs were incurred
ambiguities.resolution: Transaction states 'old $100 note replaced by new $100 note' — if fees existed, they would be mentioned. The stated amounts imply a clean replacement.

ambiguities.aspect: whether there was any gain or loss on extinguishment
ambiguities.resolution: Both notes are stated at $100 — no difference in carrying amounts means no gain or loss on extinguishment.
